title: Electronic Medical Record System Deep Review: EMRMaster vs MedicalRecordPro Feature Comparison | 2026 Review
description: Comprehensive review of electronic medical record systems, comparing medical record entry, templates, quality control and other core functions. Professional selection guide for hospital EMR implementation. Learn more about features and pricing!
content:
Electronic Medical Record System Implementation Background

Electronic medical record system is the core component of hospital informatization, directly related to medical quality, clinical efficiency, and patient safety. The National Health Commission requires hospitals to gradually achieve electronic medical record grading evaluation, EMR system selection has become a key decision for hospital informatization construction. Excellent EMR systems should support structured medical record entry, quality control, retrieval analysis and other functions, while complying with national standards and medical regulations. This article will deeply review EMRMaster and MedicalRecordPro, two major electronic medical record solutions.

Core EMR Features Comparison

Feature DimensionEMRMasterMedicalRecordProImportance Level
Structured Record EntryComprehensiveBasicCore
Record Template ManagementComprehensiveComprehensiveCore
Record Quality ControlComprehensiveBasicCore
Record Retrieval FunctionComprehensiveBasicCore
Record Statistics AnalysisComprehensiveNot SupportedImportant
Record Archive ManagementComprehensiveComprehensiveImportant
Record Security PermissionsComprehensiveComprehensiveImportant
Record Signature AuthenticationComprehensiveBasicImportant
Record Sharing TransferComprehensiveBasicImportant
Record Data ExportComprehensiveBasicImportant

EMRMaster System Details

Structured Record Entry Function

EMRMaster provides comprehensive structured medical record entry solutions, complying with national EMR standard requirements. The system supports chief complaint, present illness history, past medical history, physical examination, diagnosis, treatment plan and other record element structured entry, ensuring record content is standardized and complete. Physicians can quickly enter through templates, system automatically verifies record completeness.

  • Comprehensive record element structured entry
  • Complies with national EMR standards
  • Comprehensive template quick entry function
  • Automatic record completeness verification
  • Free text supplementary entry support
  • Intelligent prompts for record element associations

Record Quality Control Function

EMRMaster record quality control system uses intelligent review algorithms, real-time detecting record quality issues. The system supports record completeness check, standardization check, timeliness check and other multi-dimensional quality control, automatically reminding physicians to correct when issues are found. Record quality scoring system helps hospitals continuously improve record quality.

  • Intelligent review algorithm real-time detection
  • Automatic record completeness check
  • Multi-dimensional record standardization check
  • Record timeliness monitoring reminder
  • Automatic quality issue modification reminder
  • Record quality scoring statistics analysis

Record Retrieval and Analysis Function

EMRMaster record retrieval system supports multi-dimensional retrieval conditions, including patient basic information, diagnosis information, treatment information, time range etc. The system provides record content full-text retrieval function, supports complex retrieval condition combinations. Record statistics analysis function helps hospitals conduct medical quality management, research data extraction.

  • Multi-dimensional retrieval condition combinations
  • Record content full-text retrieval
  • Complex retrieval logic support
  • Quick retrieval result export
  • Comprehensive record statistics analysis tools
  • Research data extraction export support

MedicalRecordPro System Details

Structured Record Entry Function

MedicalRecordPro provides basic structured record entry function, supporting common record element entry. System structuring degree is limited, some record content still requires free text entry. Suitable for medical institutions with lower structured EMR requirements, or during EMR transition phase.

  • Basic record element structuring support
  • Common record template library
  • Some content requires free text entry
  • Basic record completeness verification
  • Suitable for transition phase use
  • Limited structuring degree

Record Quality Control Function

MedicalRecordPro record quality control provides basic check functions, mainly conducting record completeness checks. The system lacks intelligent review algorithms and multi-dimensional quality control, record quality scoring function is limited. Suitable for medical institutions with basic record quality requirements.

  • Basic record completeness check
  • Simple standardization check function
  • Lack of intelligent review algorithm
  • Limited record quality scoring function
  • Suitable for basic quality control needs
  • Basic quality issue reminder function

Record Retrieval and Analysis Function

MedicalRecordPro record retrieval function is basic, supporting patient basic information retrieval and simple diagnosis retrieval. The system lacks record content full-text retrieval and complex retrieval condition combination functions. Does not provide record statistics analysis function, suitable for simple retrieval requirement scenarios.

  • Basic patient information retrieval
  • Simple diagnosis retrieval support
  • Lack of record content full-text retrieval
  • No complex retrieval condition combinations
  • Does not provide statistics analysis function
  • Limited retrieval result export function

System Compliance Comparison

Compliance DimensionEMRMasterMedicalRecordPro
National EMR StandardsFull CompliancePartial Compliance
EMR Grading EvaluationSupports Level 5+Supports Level 3-
Record Security StandardsFull ComplianceFull Compliance
Data Encryption RequirementsComprehensive SupportComprehensive Support
Digital Signature StandardsFull ComplianceBasic Support
Record Retention Period30 Years Support15 Years Support

User Experience and Usability Comparison

EMRMaster User Experience

EMRMaster interface design is professional, function layout is reasonable and clear. Record entry workflow is smooth, template calling is quick, intelligent prompts effectively improve entry efficiency. A physician at a tertiary hospital commented: EMRMaster significantly improved record entry efficiency, quality control reminders are timely, record quality significantly improved.

MedicalRecordPro User Experience

MedicalRecordPro interface is simple and friendly, operation workflow is simple and easy to understand. Basic functions are easy to adopt, suitable for physicians first using EMR systems. A physician at a community hospital stated: MedicalRecordPro is simple and easy to use, quick to adopt, meets our record entry needs.

Price Plan Comparison Analysis

Price Plan DimensionEMRMasterMedicalRecordPro
Basic Monthly Fee$450$250
Professional Monthly Fee$700$400
Enterprise Monthly Fee$1000$600
Implementation Fee$4000 One-time$2000 One-time
Training FeeIncluded in ImplementationPer Person Pricing
Annual Maintenance Fee15% Software Fee10% Software Fee

Selection Recommendations and Recommended Scenarios

Scenarios Suitable for EMRMaster

  • Large comprehensive or specialty hospitals
  • Pursuing high-level EMR grading evaluation
  • Medical institutions with strict record quality requirements
  • Need record statistics analysis function
  • Sufficient budget for complex systems
  • Professional IT team support

Scenarios Suitable for MedicalRecordPro

  • Small-medium medical institutions or clinics
  • Basic EMR requirements sufficient
  • Limited budget pursuing cost-effectiveness
  • Need quick deployment of simple systems
  • Limited IT team capabilities
  • Suitable for transition phase use

Summary and Final Recommendation

EMR system selection should consider hospital size, EMR grading evaluation goals, quality requirements and budget. EMRMaster is suitable for large hospitals pursuing high-quality EMR and high-level grading evaluation, MedicalRecordPro is suitable for small-medium institutions with basic EMR needs. We recommend hospitals clarify EMR implementation goals before selection, conduct detailed needs analysis and trial evaluation, and choose the EMR solution that best matches their needs.
author: Healthcare IT Expert
